title: Chunking, Segmentation, Document Structure, and Identity · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible structure-aware chunking experiment with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in structure-aware chunking experiment and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit the…
objectives: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.
Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Stable Chunk Identity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-stable-chunk-identity-161wcg" that uses case 7888 to choose structure-aware chunks, bounded overlap, stable identity, and parent-child evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Stable Chunk Identity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Stable Chunk Identity into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Stable Chunk Identity, keep Rag Stable Chunk Identity connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-stable-chunk-identity-161wcg
# Competency: rag-stable-chunk-identity.
# Case 7888: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_stable_chunk_identity_161wcg(token_count: int = 920, chunk_size: int = 240, overlap: int = 40, boundary_count: int = 5, parent_linked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_161wcg = "161wcg-gcw161-7888"
    assert evidence_variant_161wcg
    evidence_axes_161wcg = (
        "corpus161wcg",
        "querygcw161",
        "tenant7888",
        "index161wcg7888",
        "promptgcw1617888",
        "evaluation7888161wcg",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_161wcg)) == 6
    if token_count <= 0 or chunk_size <= 0:
        return False, 0, "chunk-input"
    if overlap < 0 or overlap >= chunk_size:
        return False, overlap, "overlap-budget"
    if boundary_count < 1 or not parent_linked:
        return False, boundary_count, "document-structure"
    stride = chunk_size - overlap
    chunks = 1 + max(0, (token_count - chunk_size + stride - 1) // stride)
    assert chunks >= 1
    return True, chunks, "structure-aware-chunks"
